chapter_context.summary: Hushai's counsel defeats Ahithophel's plan for a quick attack, giving David time to cross the Jordan; Ahithophel hangs himself.

outline.text: Ahithophel's Counsel: Pursue David Tonight with 12000 Men
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Ahithophel's Counsel: Pursue David Tonight with 12000 Men (17:1-4), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

outline.text: Hushai's Counter-Counsel: Gather All Israel; Absalom Defeats Ahithophel's Counsel
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Hushai's Counter-Counsel: Gather All Israel; Absalom Defeats Ahithophel's Counsel (17:5-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

outline.text: Jonathan and Ahimaaz Escaped; David Warned and Crosses Jordan
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jonathan and Ahimaaz Escaped; David Warned and Crosses Jordan (17:15-22), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

outline.text: Ahithophel's Counsel Rejected; He Commits Suicide
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Ahithophel's Counsel Rejected; He Commits Suicide (17:23), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

outline.text: David at Mahanaim; Shobi, Machir, and Barzillai Bring Beds
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David at Mahanaim; Shobi, Machir, and Barzillai Bring Beds (17:24-29), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

key_themes.title: The Davidic Covenant
key_themes.description: God's unconditional promise of an eternal dynasty, ultimately pointing forward to the Messiah.

key_themes.title: Sin and Consequences
key_themes.description: While God offers complete forgiveness for sincere repentance, the earthly consequences of sin remain.

devotional.question: How do the events of Chapter 17 demonstrate the grace of God in the midst of human failure?
What lessons about leadership and accountability can you apply to your own life from this chapter?

cultural_historical.title: Jerusalem as Capital
cultural_historical.text: By capturing Jerusalem, a neutral city on the border of Judah and Benjamin, David strategically united the fractured northern and southern tribes under a single capital.
